params.description: Vertical data table appears. Favorite Fruits data. Dates: 60, Cherries: 45, Bananas: 30, Apples: 15.
Horizontal bar graph appears. Favorite Fruits data. Scale Preview System available—no scale selected yet.

beats.text: Here's data about students' favorite fruits. The most votes went to Dates with 60. Scale of 1 doesn't make sense here because it would take 60 lines on the axis. That's too much!
Your job: pick the right scale. Which scale will you use?
Explore the Scale Preview System with scales 2, 5, and 10.

validator.description: Student explored scale options freely; all three scales (2, 5, 10) show checkmarks

elements.description: Vertical data table. Favorite Fruits data. Dates: 60, Cherries: 45, Bananas: 30, Apples: 15.
Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Fruits data. Scale Preview System shows checkmarks for scales 2, 5, and 10.

params.description: Bar graph updates to Scale of 10. All bars land on tick marks.

validator.description: Student selected Scale of 2—works but not optimal for this data

beats.text: That scale could work, but check—all values end in 0 or 5. Which scale would be easiest to read?

validator.description: Student selected Scale of 5—works but not optimal for this data

beats.text: That scale could work, but check—all values end in 0 or 5. Which scale would be easiest to read?

elements.description: Vertical data table. Favorite Fruits data. Dates: 60, Cherries: 45, Bananas: 30, Apples: 15.
Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Fruits data. Scale of 10 selected. All bars visible.

beats.text: Why is Scale of 10 a good choice? Choose all of the reasons.
Why is Scale of 10 a good choice? Choose all of the reasons.
options: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.
The graph is easy to read.
The biggest number is 60 so the bars fit on the graph.
Scale of 2 takes too many marks to reach the biggest number.

and.selected: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.
The graph is easy to read.
The biggest number is 60 so the bars fit on the graph.
Scale of 2 takes too many marks to reach the biggest number.

validator.description: Student selected all four reasons, correct

beats.text: Exactly. All values are multiples of 5, so scales of 5 and 10 both work to read the graph clearly. For data going up to 60, Scale of 10 gives you a cleaner graph.

validator.description: Student did not select all four reasons

beats.text: Let's consider the questions we ask when choosing a scale. What is the biggest number? Does the data all fit? Look at all the numbers—do they end in 0 or 5 so we can find the exact location of the bars? What scale can we use for the best looking graph? All of these reasons help us choose the scale of 10.

elements.description: Vertical data table. Favorite Fruits data. Dates: 60, Cherries: 45, Bananas: 30, Apples: 15.
Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Fruits data. Scale of 10. All bars visible and landing on tick marks.
